focus: C104: K7-IGT relationship formalized. Both CEC escape conditions (K7-grounding, interpretability-grounding) unified under IGE as common mechanism. K7-grounding ⊂ interpretability-grounding ⊂ CEC escape. K7-grounding is stronger (requires IGE at both isolation verification AND evaluation). IGE-MCCF is strongest K7-grounding form. Document: state/decisions/k7-igt-relationship.md. D4 streak: 95.

igt_status.name: Interpretability-Grounding Theorem
igt_status.d4_blocked_by: DC#3 full — requires formally verifiable circuit claims + P1 prediction confirmed
igt_status.forward_prediction: P1 — circuit-verified claims replicate >85%; behavioral benchmark rankings replicate <50%
igt_status.new_insight: IGT is bridge theorem: K7 (premise) → K7-Requirement → IGE (mechanism) → IGT (theorem) → CEC escape. Two CEC escape conditions unified under IGE. K7-grounding is stronger condition (IGE at verification + evaluation); interpretability-grounding is weaker (IGE at evaluation only).

k7_igt_insight: K7-grounding requires IGE at both isolation verification AND evaluation. R-verification cannot be satisfied by behavioral inference (RAAI-DPI applies to verification, not just evaluation). IGE-MCCF at both stages = highest achievable K7-grounding. Document: state/decisions/k7-igt-relationship.md (C104).
cec_escape_structure: K7-grounding (IGE at verification + evaluation) ⊂ interpretability-grounding (IGE at evaluation) ⊂ CEC escape. IGT unifies both via IGE mechanism.
